_comment: Format registry for probe_sync.py.
Add a new entry here to support any additional data logger brand — no code changes needed.
Fields per entry:
description     Human-readable brand / software name (shown in the GUI and log).
signature       List of strings searched in the file's first ~15 lines (case-insensitive).
If ANY signature matches, this format is selected. Make signatures
distinctive enough that they won't match the wrong brand.
date_col        Column name for the date, when date and time are in SEPARATE columns.
Omit (or set null) for combined-timestamp formats.
time_col        Column name for the time (or the combined timestamp column).
value_col       Column name for the temperature reading.
dayfirst        true  = date is DD/MM/YYYY (European, e.g. EasyLog, Omega).
false = date is MM/DD/YYYY (US, e.g. MadgeTech) or YYYY-first (ISO).
Only matters for ambiguous dates where day <= 12.
datetime_format Optional explicit Python strptime string, e.g. "%d.%m.%Y %H:%M:%S".
Use this for formats that pandas cannot reliably auto-detect.
Overrides dayfirst when present.
Example — adding a hypothetical 'AcmeCorp' logger:
"acmecorp": {
"description": "AcmeCorp DataMaster Software",
"signature": ["AcmeCorp DataMaster"],
"time_col": "Timestamp",
"value_col": "Temp_C",
"datetime_format": "%d.%m.%Y %H:%M"

easylog.description: Lascar EasyLog / Omega OM-EL-USB (same software, OEM)
